items.title: USDA invites comments on SNAP utility-allowance and self-employment income reporting burden (due July 27)
items.summary: USDA’s Food and Nutrition Service published a Federal Register notice seeking comments on a revised information collection covering state options for Standard Utility Allowances (SUAs) and self-employment income calculations in SNAP. The notice estimates 53 respondents and a total annual burden of 2,131.25 hours (reporting plus recordkeeping), with comments due July 27, 2026.

items.title: USDA opens 30-day comment window on SNAP operations-change reporting and National Accuracy Clearinghouse data matching (comments due June 22)
items.summary: USDA’s Food and Nutrition Service is taking public comments on an information collection that covers (1) state reporting when making “major changes in operations” and (2) state participation in the National Accuracy Clearinghouse (NAC) to prevent duplicate SNAP issuance across states. Comments received by June 22, 2026 will be considered.

items.title: CMS publishes proposed rule on Medicaid managed care state-directed payments (public inspection May 20)
items.summary: CMS has posted a proposed rule titled “Medicaid Program; Medicaid Managed Care State Directed Payments and Medicaid Fee-For-Service Targeted Medicaid Practitioner Payments (CMS-2449-P)” for public inspection on May 20, 2026. The proposal would codify limits on Medicaid state-directed payments (SDPs) and targeted fee-for-service practitioner payments.

items.title: Education Department finalizes Workforce Pell Grant rules; eligible short-term programs can start as early as July 1
items.summary: The U.S. Department of Education released a fact sheet on its Workforce Pell Grant final rule dated May 19, 2026, with provisions for eligible workforce programs effective July 20, 2026 and an option for institutions to implement early on July 1, 2026. The rule sets program parameters of 150–599 clock hours and at least 8 but fewer than 15 weeks of instructional time, plus 70% completion and 70% employment rate requirements.

items.title: IRS launches “Trump Accounts” for children, including a $1,000 pilot contribution for eligible births in 2025–2028
items.summary: The IRS posted details on a new child savings account type called “Trump Accounts,” established under the Working Families Tax Cuts. The IRS says a $1,000 pilot contribution is available for eligible U.S.-citizen children born between Jan. 1, 2025 and Dec. 31, 2028 (with a valid SSN), and account elections are submitted through an IRS online account using Form 4547.

items.title: SSA and HHS OIG host anti-scam webinar June 4 (2:00 p.m. ET) to help beneficiaries avoid fraud
items.summary: The Social Security Administration and the HHS Office of Inspector General announced a joint webinar on Thursday, June 4, 2026 at 2:00 p.m. ET focused on recognizing, avoiding, and reporting scams and using Social Security online services safely. The event includes a Microsoft Teams join option and a dial-in audio line.

items.title: Pittsburgh housing authority closes three Project-Based Voucher waiting lists May 28 at 1:00 p.m.
items.summary: The Housing Authority of the City of Pittsburgh announced it will close three Project-Based Voucher (PBV) waiting lists effective Thursday, May 28, 2026 at 1:00 p.m. The lists are Allegheny Union Baptist Association (AUBA), Gladstone Residences, and Milliones Manor, and no pre-applications will be accepted after the deadline.
items.source: Housing Authority of the City of Pittsburgh

items.title: IRS reminder: summer day camp costs may count toward the Child and Dependent Care Credit
items.summary: In a May 26, 2026 Tax Tip, the IRS reminded families that sending a child to summer day camp may be an expense that counts toward the Child and Dependent Care Credit. The IRS also noted that taxpayers who move should file Form 8822 to update their address with the agency.
